name: dstack
tagline: Open-source control plane for GPU provisioning across clouds, Kubernetes, and on-prem
seoTitle: dstack - GPU Orchestration for ML Across Clouds
metaDescription: dstack is an open-source control plane that provisions and orchestrates GPU compute for ML development, training, and inference across clouds, Kubernetes, and on-prem.
overview: dstack is an open-source control plane for provisioning and orchestrating GPU compute. It works with any GPU cloud, Kubernetes cluster, or on-prem servers, and supports NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPU, and Tenstorrent accelerators out of the box.

It is aimed at ML and AI teams who need to run development environments, training jobs, and inference services on GPUs without managing the underlying infrastructure by hand. You describe what you want in YAML, and dstack handles provisioning, job queuing, networking, and recovery from failures.

As a compute-orchestration tool, dstack sits between your code and the hardware. It is compatible with open-source tools and frameworks, so you can keep your existing stack while letting dstack place workloads across clouds and clusters from a single interface.
features: Runs across any GPU cloud, Kubernetes cluster, or on-prem servers from one control plane
Supports NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPU, and Tenstorrent accelerators out of the box
Configuration types for fleets, dev environments, tasks, services, and volumes, all defined as YAML in your repo
Automatically handles provisioning, job queuing, auto-scaling, networking, port-forwarding, and run failures
Apply configurations via the CLI, a programmatic API, or AI agent skills
Server runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows (via WSL 2); on-prem use needs no backend config, just SSH fleets

gettingStarted.intro: Before using dstack through the CLI or API, set up a dstack server. The server runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows (via WSL 2) and requires Git and OpenSSH.

steps.heading: Install and start the server
steps.body: Install dstack with all extras and start the server. On first run it prints an admin token and the local server address.

steps.heading: Install the CLI (if not already installed)
steps.body: If the CLI was not installed alongside the server, install it on its own. It also requires Git and OpenSSH.

steps.heading: Point the CLI at your server
steps.body: Configure the CLI with the server address, your user token, and a project name.
steps.code:
dstack project add \
    --name main \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:3000 \
    --token bbae0f28-d3dd-4820-bf61-8f4bb40815da

steps.heading: Define and apply a configuration
steps.body: Define fleets, dev environments, tasks, services, or volumes as YAML in your repo, then apply them with the dstack apply command.

useCases: Spin up interactive dev environments on cloud GPUs and connect from a desktop IDE
Schedule training jobs, including distributed jobs, across GPU clouds or Kubernetes
Deploy models and web apps as services with auto-scaling and authorization
Orchestrate on-prem GPU clusters as SSH fleets without configuring a cloud backend
